eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the operationalized framework model.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to MongoDB Minimum Correctness Configuration (3-node replica set: 1 primary + 2 secondary)

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Prudential technology-level estimate for a minimal 3-node replica set without sharding, used only for framework classification. The value is not a benchmark of the MCC but an order-of-magnitude estimate consistent with MongoDB's general-purpose database architecture.
ps_throughput.source: MongoDB replication architecture and replica set operational model

eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: MongoDB self-managed database model: no native protocol transaction fee

eco_storage_footprint.source: Framework baseline storage assumption for MongoDB MCC

parallel_execution.motivation: MongoDB supports concurrent execution of operations and general-purpose database workloads.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: MongoDB supports horizontal partitioning through sharding, but this capability is not native to the minimal replica-set architecture considered in the framework baseline and therefore does not characterize the base technology configuration used for comparison.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: MongoDB does not rely on a globally serialized ledger ordering model.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: In the non-sharded replica-set architecture, write coordination remains centered on a primary node, so coordination is not structurally elastic at the technology baseline considered by the framework.

append_only_structural_history.motivation: MongoDB records are mutable and the database is not structurally append-only at application-state level.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: MongoDB does not natively provide ledger-style cryptographic chaining between records.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: MongoDB provides authentication and authorization, but not intrinsic cryptographic attestation of each record write as a structural ledger property.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: There is no trustless independent multi-party verification model comparable to blockchain/DLT systems.

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: MongoDB does not provide protocol-enforced irreversible commit in the ledger sense; rollback scenarios remain possible.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: With majority write concern, commit acknowledgment depends on multiple data-bearing members.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: MongoDB documentation explicitly describes rollback scenarios in replica set failover conditions.

rci_finality_rollback_resistance.source: MongoDB rollback and write concern model

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.configuration_note: Score refers strictly to MCC with 3 data-bearing members in a standard replica set.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: Only the primary receives write operations in a replica set.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: Replica set elections and write availability depend on majority/quorum semantics.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: Replica sets provide automatic failover via election of a new primary.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: A standard three-member replica set provides redundancy and write continuity if quorum is preserved.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.source: MongoDB replication and replica set architecture documentation

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: Replica sets are normally controlled administratively by a single organization.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Replication across nodes does not constitute independent multi-entity validation.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Node membership is configured and controlled by the operator; there is no permissionless or independent validator admission.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The set of replica set members is explicitly administered and fixed by configuration choices.

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: MongoDB supports authentication and role-based authorization.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: MongoDB supports managed user identities and access-control configuration for deployments.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: Identity governance remains under operator control and is not structurally decentralized across independent entities.

ags_collusion_resilience.configuration_note: Score reflects technical majority semantics in a replica set, not decentralized governance across independent organizations.

non_unilateral_compromise_possible.motivation: The deployment is typically under one administrative domain, so unilateral organizational compromise remains plausible.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Elections and stronger write guarantees rely on majority semantics among data-bearing members.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: MongoDB replica set semantics do not require supermajority thresholds.

ags_collusion_resilience.source: MongoDB replica set write concern and election model
